metric_value: PHI began as a cellar operation in 1959 with four 'whiz kids' applying technical theory to specific problems; brochure-stated; Kenney book says 1958
PHI grew to one of leading full-service data processing companies in the nation, providing services to 400+ clients across three operating divisions
PHI 'third in the nation' per back-cover recruiting ad
Philip Hankins founded PHI; later research showed he had programmed parts of Apollo moon project navigation
notes: Kastner: 'I was truly working with rocket scientists.'

metric_value: Wang Laboratories acquired PHI in 1968
Kastner started at PHI March 1969 as junior programmer at $7,200/year
Bank vice presidents earned ~$6,500; Kastner's $7,200 programmer salary exceeded VP salaries — 'titles are cheap'
CPU time was $360/CPU-hour at PHI; 20 hours equaled Kastner's annual pay; computer time was precious, programmer time was not
Kastner hired to fix PHI internal accounting system that was costing thousands of dollars/month in CPU time
PHI billed customers by I/O calls (not elapsed time) using SMF; Kastner discovered MVT consumed significant billable time itself
Kastner combined six PHI accounting COBOL programs into two with internal sorts, cutting operating costs by half (per ADL bio)
PHI data center systems programmers taught Kastner JCL, I/O minimization, dump reading; apprenticeship was main path
notes: No databases, project-management tools, or debuggers besides memory dumps.

metric_value: After 90 days, Kastner promoted from junior programmer to programmer/analyst in PHI Professional Services Division
PHI brochure describes Kastner as 'Systems Analyst with PSD's Financial Systems Group' working on PHI internal accounting and First Chicago correspondent-bank DDA
Kastner programmed correspondent-bank DDA system for First Chicago covering 14 correspondents with custom-tailored apps based on run-time parameters and flexible model software in COSMOS
notes: Online check-processing front-end interface; validation/update portions.

metric_value: Kastner designed/programmed retail gas-station accounting system in IBM COBOL; daily cash transmittal proved to pump meter readings
Kastner designed bond-portfolio analysis changes allowing 30-year bonds traded in 1970 — early Y2K-precursor
Kastner designed modifications to PHI Payroll for online batch entry plus concurrent master-file processing for client with 460 customers
PHI PSD composed of four groups: Commercial Programming, Financial Systems, Systems Analysis & Design, Systems Programming
Group developed PHI Telecommunications Programming System (TPS), regarded as most comprehensive front-end software for IBM telecom environment; plug-replacement for IBM 2700-series TCU
notes: Operating at PHI Data Center and several locations.

metric_value: PSD operated 'PHI Interface Laboratory' for testing System/360 channel interfaces for Honeywell H-516, Bunker Ramo, Data General Nova, DEC PDP-11, Tempo 1; nicknamed 'Underwriters Laboratory of the 360 Interface'
PHI developed tape/disk utilities for IBM System/360 Model 20 provided as Class A support by IBM
PHI developed real-time operating system for Sanders SANDAC 200 communications processor
PHI developed System/360-resident simulator for HP 2100 series
PHI R&D group helped develop software for new Wang 3300 computer series using third-generation computer technology
CRT version of PHI Airline Terminal Support Package operating at Texas International Airlines for over six months
Data Measurements Corporation licensed special version of PHI Airline Terminal Support Package supporting 32/48/64 DMC 201 or IBM 1977 terminals from 8K Honeywell H-316
PHI announced PHITAX packaged module for Federal/State/local payroll-tax deductions; isolated tax tables for simplified maintenance; written in COBOL
James Lees promoted to Manager of Proprietary Products at PHI
John Morse promoted to Manager of PSD Systems Programming Group
John Pryke promoted to Sales Manager for PSD
Former Honeywell employee Leo Fleck joined PHI as Senior Consultant directing PSD's newly formed consulting service for Honeywell Large Scale Systems users
